skill.name: portfolio-context
skill.description: Provides project context for Portfolio Buddy 2 development, including the React stack, trading analytics features, known bugs, and architecture rules.
skill.summary: Guide Portfolio Buddy 2 development with current project constraints and feature context.
skill.author: 5MinFutures

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found only Markdown inline code, a directory tree, and descriptive portfolio terminology. No executable code, shell invocation, cryptographic implementation, network access, filesystem access, secret handling, or prompt injection attempt was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code Misread as Commands
low_findings.description: The flagged text uses backticks to format TypeScript terms and repository paths in documentation. It is not Ruby or shell backtick execution, and no command is executed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is plain Markdown prose and inline code formatting. There is no executable script context or interpolation target.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Code Fence Misread as Commands
low_findings.description: The flagged code fence documents the project directory layout. It contains filenames only and does not define or invoke executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are Markdown fence delimiters around a static directory listing. They do not contain command syntax or runtime behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Pattern in Documentation
low_findings.description: The flagged lines describe portfolio analysis context and Sortino variance correction. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithm is used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are descriptive text about development context and statistical variance. I did not find evidence of cryptographic code or configuration.

content.value_statement: Portfolio Buddy 2 development needs consistent project memory across React, TypeScript, and trading analytics work. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code the project context needed for safer implementation decisions.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes the Portfolio Buddy 2 tech stack and supported libraries.
Identifies known technical debt in React components and TypeScript types.
Documents trading analytics features such as Sortino ratio and date filtering.
Explains architecture rules for hooks, utilities, state, and component size.
Maps important source areas for portfolio, metrics, upload, and correlation features.
Provides migration context for remaining work from the older application.
limitations: It contains static project notes and does not inspect the current repository state.
It does not run tests, linting, builds, or database checks by itself.
It is specific to Portfolio Buddy 2 and is not a general portfolio analytics framework.
Some line counts and known issues may become stale as the codebase changes.

use_cases.title: Plan React Refactors
use_cases.description: Use the documented component limits and known oversized files to scope refactoring work before editing.

use_cases.title: Maintain Trading Metrics
use_cases.description: Use the project notes to preserve Sortino, Sharpe, correlation, and contract multiplier behavior during changes.

use_cases.title: Review Migration Progress
use_cases.description: Use the migration context and feature list to align new tasks with the remaining Portfolio Buddy 2 roadmap.

prompt_templates.title: Understand the Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the Portfolio Buddy 2 stack, main features, known issues, and architecture constraints from this skill.

prompt_templates.title: Scope a Small Fix
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused bug or tech debt task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the Portfolio Buddy 2 context, identify the likely files and constraints for fixing this issue: [describe issue].

prompt_templates.title: Refactor a Feature Area
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a refactor for [component or hook] while respecting the 200-line component limit, strict TypeScript, and existing hook patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Analytics Changes
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of financial metric behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this proposed change to portfolio metrics for correctness, affected files, and risks to Sortino, correlation, date filtering, and contract multipliers.

output_examples.input: Where should I add a portfolio date filter improvement?
output_examples.output: The likely starting point is the portfolio hook because date filtering is implemented there. Check related chart and table components for display impact.

output_examples.input: What should I watch for when editing PortfolioSection?
output_examples.output: Keep the Sortino logic intact, avoid adding more size to the oversized component, and consider extracting focused subcomponents.

output_examples.input: How should I handle new metric calculations?
output_examples.output: Prefer pure utility functions when possible, preserve strict TypeScript types, and verify downstream table, chart, and correlation behavior.

best_practices: Pair this context with a fresh repository inspection before editing files.
Use the documented architecture rules to keep hooks, utilities, and components separated.
Recheck financial metric formulas and chart behavior after analytics changes.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the stored line counts as authoritative after code changes.
Do not add new state management libraries without a project decision.
Do not move portfolio-level calculations without checking existing feature context.

faq.question: What project is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is for Portfolio Buddy 2, a React and TypeScript portfolio analysis tool for investors and futures traders.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown context file and does not include scripts or executable commands.

faq.question: Can it calculate financial metrics by itself?
faq.answer: No. It documents where and how metrics are handled in the project, but it does not run calculations.

faq.question: Which AI tools does it support?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is the context always current?
faq.answer: No. It should be checked against the live repository because project files and line counts can change.

faq.question: Why does it mention Supabase and Cloudflare?
faq.answer: Those are part of the documented Portfolio Buddy 2 backend and deployment stack.
